steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Form, Structure, and Sense question testing verb tense. The word "Currently" at the start of the sentence signals that the action is happening right now, in the present. The correct verb must use simple present tense to match that time frame.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — includes: Simple present tense, which correctly expresses a present-time fact established by "Currently." ✓
  • Choice B — included: Simple past tense, which places the action in the past and contradicts "Currently." ✗
  • Choice C — will have included: Future perfect tense, which describes an action completed at some future point — inconsistent with "Currently." ✗
  • Choice D — had included: Past perfect tense, which describes an action completed before another past event — inconsistent with "Currently." ✗
That matches Choice A — includes.
